title: calandrinia oblongifolia var. humilis
intro: Calandrinia oblongifolia var. humilis, commonly known as the low-growing pink purslane, is a low-growing succulent plant species native to Western Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Calandrinia oblongifolia var. humilis include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate digestive issues and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, Calandrinia oblongifolia var. humilis is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to benefit from its medicinal properties.
